extraction.one_line_summary: EVENEPOEL won the final stage from a two-man break with ROGLIČ, but ROGLIČ defended GC by 6 seconds after Quick-Step burned all teammates on the circuits.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step's pace on Montjuïc circuits shredded EVENEPOEL's entire team, forcing him to attack solo and collaborate with race leader.
ALMEIDA finished ahead of teammate YATES suggests UAE Emirates lacked cohesion in protecting third overall on final stage circuits.
Breakaways held on five of nine historical editions yet peloton teams ignored cooperation, allowing EVENEPOEL-ROGLIČ duo to escape.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: forced to attack alone when teammates were dropped, then won stage from two-up against race leader
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: matched EVENEPOEL's attack despite defending GC, refused to cooperate with chasers holding 10-second margin
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: positive: finished fourth on stage and third overall ahead of own team leader YATES
rider_signals.YATES Adam: negative: beaten by teammate ALMEIDA for third overall suggests he lacked strength on Montjuïc circuits
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: neutral: bridged to leaders but worked for ALMEIDA rather than disrupting EVENEPOEL-ROGLIČ collaboration
rider_signals.HAYTER Ethan: neutral: made original breakaway but caught as Quick-Step tempo destroyed early move
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: neutral: attempted attacks from break for mountains points but failed to sustain effort
rider_signals.DE LA CRUZ David: neutral: last original breakaway survivor before EVENEPOEL-ROGLIČ move, showed persistence on circuits
